# services.pulseaudio

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.pulseaudio`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.pulseaudio.configFile` | `null or absolute path` | The path to the default configuration options the PulseAudio server should use. By default, the "default.pa" configuration from the PulseAudio distribution is used. |
| `services.pulseaudio.daemon.config` | `attribute set of unspecified value` | Config of the pulse daemon. See `man pulse-daemon.conf`. |
| `services.pulseaudio.daemon.logLevel` | `string` | The log level that the system-wide pulseaudio daemon should use, if activated. |
| `services.pulseaudio.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the PulseAudio sound server. |
| `services.pulseaudio.extraClientConf` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra configuration appended to pulse/client.conf file. |
| `services.pulseaudio.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Literal string to append to `configFile` and the config file generated by the pulseaudio module. |
| `services.pulseaudio.extraModules` | `list of package` | Extra pulseaudio modules to use. This is intended for out-of-tree pulseaudio modules like extra bluetooth codecs. Extra modules take precedence over built-in pulseaudio modules. |
| `services.pulseaudio.package` | `package` | The PulseAudio derivation to use. This can be used to enable features (such as JACK support, Bluetooth) via the `pulseaudioFull` package. |
| `services.pulseaudio.support32Bit` | `boolean` | Whether to include the 32-bit pulseaudio libraries in the system or not. This is only useful on 64-bit systems and currently limited to x86_64-linux. |
| `services.pulseaudio.systemWide` | `boolean` | If false, a PulseAudio server is launched automatically for each user that tries to use the sound system. The server runs with user privileges. If true, one system-wide PulseAudio server is launched on boot, running as the user "pulse", and only users in the "pulse-access" group will have access to the server. Please read the PulseAudio documentation for more details. Don't enable this option unless you know what you are doing. |
| `services.pulseaudio.tcp.anonymousClients.allowAll` | `boolean` | Whether to enable all anonymous clients to stream to the server. |
| `services.pulseaudio.tcp.anonymousClients.allowedIpRanges` | `list of string` | A list of IP subnets that are allowed to stream to the server. |
| `services.pulseaudio.tcp.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable tcp streaming support. |
| `services.pulseaudio.tcp.openFirewall` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Open firewall for the specified port. |
| `services.pulseaudio.tcp.port` | `null or 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | TCP connection port. The default `null` value, means pulseaudio will try to use the default 4713 port, but if it is occupied, it will fallback to a random port. |
| `services.pulseaudio.zeroconf.discovery.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable discovery of pulseaudio sinks in the local network. |
| `services.pulseaudio.zeroconf.publish.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable publishing the pulseaudio sink in the local network. |
